site.title: The Semester
site.subtitle: ten envelopes, one story, kept for you

envelopes.title: Envelope 1
envelopes.signOff: — to be continued, Episode 2 is coming

paragraphs.text: Hi,

This is Tuesday, 15 August 2025. It's a sweet evening time on the back road of my college. The sun is going down and I and my friend Wasim, we were going for playing basketball and we are talking about the new students which have come recently, the 3rd batch of the NFSU Dharwad. And we are on the back road of my college main building and on that road there is a T-point. And before that T-point there is a jump, and me and Wasim are about 10 meters away from that jump and then from another side there is an auto-rickshaw coming. And as the rickshaw slows down to get over the jump, I saw something magical in the rickshaw. This is a damn beautiful girl; is she in the rickshaw smiling with joy? And beside her 2 persons are also there, maybe her parents. This is the new girl which is joining our college, and me and Wasim look at each other and somehow I said, “Boht sundar hai yaar”.

highlights.phrase: And as the rickshaw slows down to get over the jump, I saw something magical in the rickshaw. This is a damn beautiful girl; is she in the rickshaw smiling with joy?

paragraphs.text: And then we all friends met at the dinner table and Wasim said, “Bhai, kya sundar ladki dekhi hai yaar, itni sundar.” Then Mridul asked, “Kaun thi woh?” Then I said, “Humein kya pata, par sundar boht thi ek dam mast.” Then all are looking at me and start smiling and then Mridul asked, “Kaise dikhti hai woh?” Then I said, “Are boht sundar hai yaar, thoda patla sa muh hai thodi golu molu si.” Then Borade said, “Dhoondhte hai kaun hai.” Then the search began…

For the reference, we are the 7 friends. 3 you already met, now the next 4: Yash (we call him Rao), Aditya (we call him Adi), and V. Srinivas (we call him Sinu). Now the search began and we started to search for that girl and we decided that the best option to find that girl is to sit in the mess and look at the new girls coming to the campus. So we started to sit in the mess for a long time, and I have hypermetropia so I can’t be able to see long-distance things, so I can’t be able to correctly judge how she is. Everyone was asking me, "Is she there?" I said no, and then one day Wasim came to me and said, "Bhai mil gayi." I said, "Kahan hai? Bata bata." And we were happy. And then now the second step: what is her name? So one day when we saw that they are going to play something, we saw them in the sports room and Wasim went after them and searched the book and found that her name is Anjali, and he came to us and said, "Bhai uska naam Anjali hai."

Then the day came when I saw her on Ganesh Chaturthi on 25 Aug 2025. She was in a yellow sari and I saw her that second time and, “Whaa!” Her yellow sari and then her damn cutie face and her beautiful eyes and her curly hairs. One thing I really liked most in her is her eyes. I got her first eye contact and I kept on removing that eye contact because I was not in good clothes, and Mridul kept on saying, “Dekh dekh vahi hai na dekh rahi hai.” “Aur main thoda thoda sharmata chala gaya.” Then that day many times I got eye contact with her. I was getting happy inside and started to say, “Thank you God.”

highlights.phrase: I saw her that second time and, “Whaa!” Her yellow sari and then her damn cutie face and her beautiful eyes and her curly hairs. One thing I really liked most in her is her eyes. I got her first eye contact and I kept on removing that eye contact because I was not in good clothes, and Mridul kept on saying, “Dekh dekh vahi hai na dekh rahi hai.” “Aur main thoda thoda sharmata chala gaya.” Then that day many times I got eye contact with her. I was getting happy inside and started to say, “Thank you God.”

paragraphs.text: We had great news from Mridul after a couple of days: “Uska naam Anjali nahi hai, uska naam Nainsi hai aur woh Haryana se hai aur woh B.Sc. ki hai,” and “hum sab khush.”

And during the period of Ganesh Chaturthi, I got fever and the flu, so I wasn’t even able to move. During that time we got to know that they became 2 good friends: one is Anjali and the second one is my girl, Nainsi, and then…

One day we thought, "Let’s go and talk to them," and we got the info that Anjali is from Lucknow. So Wasim went and talked to her and also introduced me to Nainsi. And then we started to talk about a nice plan. We both were going towards them on that day; she was wearing some blue kind of top and we were on the way to meet them. We knew that they were on the badminton court, some function of Ganesh Chaturthi was going on there. And what we saw, there were 2 persons already talking to them and they had already begun the talk with them. So Wasim said, “Chal na main baat karta hoon.” And then I said, “Rehne de bhai mat kar bezzati ho jayegi, chhod.” And we moved on, saw the function, and got back to the room. After that, days kept going and we kept on seeing that girl and hoping to talk to them, but all day those boys were with them.

Then the days passed and the mid-semester exams were about to start. The exams got over and we were about to go back home for the Diwali break, and then Rao said, “Bhai karna hai toh karle, abhi time hai, baat karke aaja, yahi time hai.” I thought, “Karna toh chahiye.” Then I started asking, “Kaise baat karoon and what to ask them, kya boloon?” And then I got the thing, prepared the plan and made the lines for her. This was my first time and I asked my brother how to approach her; he also gave me the lines and then we had the perfect paragraph to give to her. And then planning to meet her on the date 8 Nov 2025, and I was on the mess road of the college walking and waiting for her, and I saw her coming and… Episode 2 is coming!

Now, as time started to spend in the college, we just had some beautiful eye contact. Now the final day has come. I have my line, I just need to say it. I have played basketball, I have had dinner, and now I was just waiting for her so that I can say it to her. After dinner, I started to search for her and I found out she was sitting near her hostel with her friend. I said to Wasim, “Bhai dekh, jab vo log chalna shuru karenge tab hum bhi chalenge, tab tak isi road pe rehte hain.”

Then I saw they started to walk, and they were walking in the same lane as we were. Suddenly, they changed lanes as we came close to them; that’s how they did it two to three times. Then I said to Wasim, “Bhai dekh, inko baat nahi karni. Tu yahi ruk, main akela jaata hoon. Agar hua toh baat kar loonga.” Then I left Wasim and started for the last attempt, and finally, I got the chance. I said, “Excuse me,” and she said, “Haan.”

Then I said, “Aap kaha se ho?” Then her friend beside her started to stare at me. I said to her, “2 min please.” Then she said, “Haryana.” Then I started to say my lines: “I want to tell you that I have a crush on you. I found you an interesting person, and you are very pretty. Can we go on a walk?” As soon as I said that, she started to look here and there, and then I said, “You can take your time,” and then I moved back and went toward the badminton court. My friends were there, and as I reached there, everyone was asking what happened. Before I was about to, I said, “Bhai, ya toh win hoga ya learn hoga.” Then Mridul, Rao, and Adi—everyone was asking what happened. I said, “Maine aisa bola, poora bataya, aur bola, ‘Take your time,’ bolke aa gaya.” Then I said, “Ab pata nahi win hoga ya learn hoga.” Then Mridul said, “Reaction kya tha?” I said, “Has rahi thi, main ‘Take your time’ bolke aa gaya.” Then Mridul said, “Sahi hai, win hai, win hai!”

highlights.phrase: hen I said, “Aap kaha se ho?” Then her friend beside her started to stare at me. I said to her, “2 min please.” Then she said, “Haryana.” Then I started to say my lines: “I want to tell you that I have a crush on you. I found you an interesting person, and you are very pretty. Can we go on a walk?”

paragraphs.text: And then we all went to the hostel and had a nice sleep. Then the second day happened; we were waiting for something she would say. Then 3rd day, then 4th day. Finally, I thought, “Apne ko hi jana chahiye, poochke aate hain.” Then on that day, me and my friends were standing on that front road of the mess and we waited for around 1 hour, and then she came from the mess. Everyone said, “Ja, ja, baat karke aa.” Before I went, I had the lines I had prepared again—same process—but this time Borade added something. He said to me, “Agar tu kuch bole aur woh peeche jaane lage, toh samajh lena na hai.” Basically, he gave me the knowledge of boy language. Now, I was on my way to speak to her. As I reached, I said, “Nainsi, baat karni hai.” As I said this much, she turned and started saying, “Nahi, nahi, baat nahi karni.” And she became tensed and started being frustrated—maybe she had a fight with someone or her mood was not right. I saw her legs; she was going backwards, and I got my answer. Then I said, “Okay, okay,” and came back. All this happened in less than 60 seconds, and then my friend asked what happened: “Itna jaldi kyun aa gaya?” And I said, “Bhai, learn hai, learn hai, mana kar diya.” That I got rejected for the 3rd time. To be honest, that day I felt very, very bad, and my chest became heavy because I genuinely liked her very much. And I didn't tell anybody; I was just reacting normally as if nothing happened. Then the night ended.

highlights.phrase: of boy language. Now, I was on my way to speak to her. As I reached, I said, “Nainsi, baat karni hai.” As I said this much, she turned and started saying, “Nahi, nahi, baat nahi karni.” And she became tensed and started being frustrated—maybe she had a fight with someone or her mood was not right. I saw her legs; she was going backwards, and I got my answer. Then I said, “Okay, okay,” and came back. All this happened in less than 60 seconds, and then my friend asked what happened: “Itna jaldi kyun aa gaya?” And I said, “Bhai, learn hai, learn hai, mana kar diya.” That I got rejected for the 3rd time

paragraphs.text: Before sleep, my roommate came to me and asked, “Bhai, aaj kisi ladki ko propose karke aaya hai kya? Kya usne tujhe mana kar diya?” I said, “Nahi, bhai.” And the next morning as I got into the mess, everyone was staring at me. I was like, “Bhai, bezzati ho gayi.” During lunch time, one of my juniors, Vaibhav, came to me and said, “Bhaiya, yeh kya kar diya? Aise nahi karna chahiye na.” I said, “Bhai, jo tu sun raha hai woh galat hai.” And then every day I used to hear all this... some, some have done this... Episode 3 coming soon.
hiii,

The mid-semester break began, and I went home. I had the thought that when I returned, everyone would forget what had happened. Before the semester break, one day, we went to a viewpoint in Dharwad—I am forgetting the name, but there was a temple there. As soon as I got there, the first thing I requested from God was, “Bhagwan, Nainsi bahut achi ladki hai and I really like her, mujhe bahut pasand hai, please Bhagwan, please.” Then, I repeated the same three lines that I usually said to God, and when I reached home, I had to report the same thing to my mind. There was no change.

Soon, the mid-semester break ended, and we got back to college. As the semester began, one of the weird things we started to see was that these two girls, Nainsi and her friend, started staying away from us. For example, if my friends and I were going to the mess, they would be coming out of it and would change their lane.

One day, when we were all going to college, they were coming towards us. In Dharwad, to protect against the heat, students often used umbrellas. She had one, and as soon as we approached them, she tilted the umbrella towards us as she passed by. We all wondered, "What happened, yaar? I didn't even do anything that should make her behave so weirdly." Then I thought, "Yaar, if they don't like it and are angry, then I shouldn't even look at them." But then, I thought, "Yaar, let's try something so we can at least talk again."

After a few days had passed, I lost hope. However, after a long gap of a couple of weeks, Rishabh and I were taking something from the college canteen—we had taken ice cream. They both came there, and after taking our ice cream, we went outside while they went inside. I looked at the ice cream and said to Rishabh, "Bhai, main vanilla leke aaya," because we usually bring chocolate, and I don't like chocolate. I went there and saw she was taking ice cream as well. As soon as she saw me, she moved backward. I said to her, "Tum le lo," and she waved her hand, showing an action for me to proceed. I took it and went from there, but one thing I noticed was that she smiled. That was the first day I saw her smiling. First, I was in love with her eyes, and now I was also in love with her smile. I was damn happy that day.

highlights.phrase: because we usually bring chocolate, and I don't like chocolate. I went there and saw she was taking ice cream as well. As soon as she saw me, she moved backward. I said to her, "Tum le lo," and she waved her hand, showing an action for me to proceed. I took it and went from there, but one thing I noticed was that she smiled. That was the first day I saw her smiling. First, I was in love with her eyes, and now I was also in love with her smile. I was damn happy that day.

paragraphs.text: After that, I didn't get a chance to talk, so I tried to create opportunities so she might be impressed and give me a chance. One thing was that I started wearing good clothes. During that time, I was applying for an internship in Indore. One day, there was a photo shoot for our batch, and we had to wear formal clothing. I thought, "Black and white—I look smart in black and white." In the morning, I was searching for her so she could look at me and think, "Acha toh lagta hai." But the morning shoot got canceled, and I was like, "Gayi bhas pani mein, ho gaya satyanash kapdon ka." The college administration said the shoot would happen in the evening after lunch. I was happy because, in the lunch break, I had to dress up again to go to the mess. I identified her, but then I got a call from my father, so I started talking to him in front of her to get some chance to make her look at me. But "baat karte waqt awaaz nahi aayi, shor itna tha mess mein, toh saari hawa-baazi khatam." I moved out of the mess to talk, and as I got back, she had already gone.

In the evening, as we were doing the photo shoot, I saw they were sitting on the left side of the main building. Consequently, my friends also went and stood in front of them. I thought I would get out of the group so I could look at her and she would look at me, but that attempt also failed—she didn't look at me. The day ended with no "hawa-baazi." I thought the sports week would be a good chance to talk, so in the month of January, when the teams were being distributed, I told my captain, "Please take her." My thought was that I could talk to her by playing a game. The captain took her into our team, but the day of the sports event arrived, and we got to know she wasn't playing any game, so that plan also flopped. I tried many times to get her to play, but she denied it. I then thought, "Bhai, iske saath toh nahi hoga apna."

Then Holi came, and I thought, "This is the chance, I will get to talk to her." The day came, but the college said there was no Holi celebration because there was no plan before. However, somehow everything was arranged, and the college announced they would celebrate Holi after lunch. Having lunch, we came before everyone. Mridul started to say, "Let's shoot the reel." We shot the reel and saw her and her friend at the gate of their hostel. Soon, we started playing Holi. I was afraid of doing anything because she would definitely shout at me, but somehow we started playing with the water. She was filling up the buckets, so I took the buckets, poured water on her, and said, "Happy Holi." I went away and then came again; this time she poured water on me and said, "Happy Holi." I took the tray and poured color on them. After that, I said to Mridul, "Tu laga ke aa ja meri jagah." She did that, and she put color on her. I got eye contact, but I couldn't speak or do anything. The day ended; we brought bhang that day, we took it, and went to sleep. Then, after a few days, something happened.
hiii

It is 17 March 2026. After dinner, I went to bed, and I just opened the Instagram messages. As always, it was empty, but there was a '1' showing on the request tab. I opened it and had a message from someone saying,

Then that night, I asked Rao, “Bhai, ek aur message aaya hai, kya karna hai?” He said, “Karde, dekhte hain kaun.” I asked this to him because someone had played a prank on me before—like someone sent an anonymous message, and I got into trouble—so I asked him whether I should reply or not. And then I replied. Now, this time, my motive was to find out who he or she is. For that, we kept on writing messages to her to keep finding things out that we could form the chat around. We asked questions of her because she told us that she is a girl, so the first hint was that this is a girl. Then we kept asking questions to her to find out, but we didn't get anything that could make any progress. Then we, meaning all my friends, were working on finding that person, and then we found the common connection between the two groups of girls—the girl called Aswari was the first with another group of girls, and I got the anonymous message. But this time, we were with the group of girls in which Nainsi was there, so from there, I got to know that she is the one. This time, Nainsi made the message; we were not sure, so we made multiple attempts. Like, when they were nearby, we called on that messaging app to find out if they would pick up or not, but that time we failed. Then I kept on asking questions, “How do you know me? What do you know about me?” And at that time, the mid-semester exams were going on, so in the evening, the talking started with, “How was your paper?” and all. During that investigation, we did something: we messaged them when they were in the mess, and if we saw that they were there, then they would message me after moving out of the mess, and the same thing happened. After the mid-semester exams were over, I said, “Listen, tell me your ID or who you are, or we just stop talking.” And then something happened: I got the request from my girl Nainsi, and I was damn happy about that. I needed to give the update to my friends as well that I got the message, and after that, we started having the conversation on a regular basis, and then we shared our numbers. At first, I was a bit introverted because this time I was talking to a girl for this long time.
Then we started talking on a regular basis, and one day I messaged her, “Let's meet,” and she said yes, and then we met.and then the story bagin

envelopes.title: Envelope 5
envelopes.signOff: to be continued, Episode 6 is coming

paragraphs.text: hiii

Now finally, the day arrived where I got the chance to meet her. She showed up wearing a pink top—this was around 7:00 PM—and she seemed just as shy as I was. But that day, I was busy pretending to be more confident than I felt. I said, “Hii kese hai?” She just kept smiling, and I think she didn’t even answer at first. I asked what happened, and she said, “Nothing,” and then followed up with, “Tum chalo m aati hu.” She called her friend along just to create a bit of a comfort zone for herself. I asked her again, “Chale?” and she said okay. We started walking until her friend arrived, and then it was just us. I was saying, “Thank God,” in my head, and I was so incredibly nervous because I didn’t know what to say. Somehow, we started talking about the anonymous message story, the college, and a bit of her background. This was the first time for us, and she was as nervous as I was, but somehow we talked a lot.
During this time, I already had my ticket to go home on May 12, 2026.

And soon, we started those late-night conversations. I was very bad at chatting, and since I’d never had a girlfriend, I wasn’t in the habit of texting for long hours. Sometime, what would happen is I’d see the “typing” bubble and I’d wait for her message, thinking, “2 second aakhe band kar letaa hu.” Those two seconds would turn into 8 or 9 hours of sleep. As soon as I woke up in the morning, I’d see a ton of messages from her: “Sogaya?”, “Sogya kya kutee?”, and “Kesa hai so jata hai.” We had such a beautiful time; she used to send me sweet reels and everything was going normally. She’d ask something, I’d answer, and then I’d ask something back. This went on until she asked me about my fantasies. I checked that again, and she said, “What is your fantasy?” That was the time we really started getting into each other and having fun talks. During these chats, she started sending me beautiful, sweet messages like “I love you,” but I didn’t respond. I don’t know why—I was confused about how things were moving this fast. How could someone like me this much? “Itne salo m tho kese ko nahi aaya pasand.” I started to get scared and began creating some distance. I feared how much this person was loving me and wondered how I could ever return that love, so I avoided those messages and changed the direction of the chat. She used to say, “Sir kabhi apni feeling share karoge?” One thing I’d do when we were walking or chatting deeply—like when “I love you” or feelings came up—is I’d express my feelings and then immediately say, “M tho mazak kar raha tha.” I thought she’d understand I was loving her. Aside from that, we had great conversations; we were both happy to spend time together.

And then we went on our first date to Rajatai Dhaba, which is a favorite place of mine because I get the dal bati that I love. As we were crossing the road, I reached for her hand—I moved my hand toward hers—but she refused to take it. That day, her right eye was having some trouble; I think something had fallen in it. I told her to get some water, but she refused that as well. She was wearing a black kurti and looked extremely beautiful. We were waiting for the magic to happen, but it wasn't coming easily. I thought, “Yrr ku bezti kara raha hai aa ja na.” Soon enough, the magic arrived. We reached the dhaba and ordered four dal bati and churma. I told her the cold coffee there was very good and asked if she wanted some, so I took her to the cold coffee spot. It’s her favorite drink. We talked about so many things, getting to know each other well, and had a wonderful time.

Then suddenly one day, I got a message from someone named Alpha. He asked, “Who are you? What is going on with Nainsi and why are you walking with her?” When I asked her about it, she explained that Alpha was her ex and told me his story. While we were walking, she said I was hers and I could do whatever I wanted. She asked, “Do you love me?” That was the moment I really had to think about whether I loved her or not. I was getting more confused because I loved spending time with her and sharing everything with her. When she asked again, “Do you love me or aise hi time pass kar rahe ho?”, I got scared. She asked twice, and finally I replied, “Karta hu yrr,” in a very low voice. Then I told her I was confused and explained that while I liked her, I needed time to sort things out. So, we went back to being us.

After that, many messages came from his side saying, “Don't move around her.” He started sending me voice notes about their past story, telling me not to be with her. One day he called Nainsi, and we tried to find out who was leaking information to him. Eventually, we decided to ignore him and move forward. This was one of the problems we faced together, and that day I thought, “She is the one, my girl.” But I still wasn't completely confident and was still too introverted to tell her everything. She was much more expressive than I was.
Then we went on our second date to Domino's. That day we didn't have much to do—it was Sunday and I wanted pizza, so we went there, had our pizza, and that was it.

Soon the end-semester exams arrived. I asked her when she was going home, and she said the evening of the 12th. I told her I was leaving in the morning. She mentioned it would be difficult being apart. During that time, I got confirmation for my internship in Indore starting in late August. she was worried that the summer would be hard and said, “Don't have the good experience with long distance.” I told her nothing would happen. She kept saying she would miss me, and I kept giving her my usual answer: that I don't miss anybody in my life. The exams happened, and I changed my ticket from the 12th to the 8th without telling her. I thought I’d just surprise her. I don't know why I did it, but I did. She had plans for us to spend time together after the exams finished on the 8th, but I destroyed her plan and boarded the train on May 8, 2025, four days before her.

envelopes.title: Envelope 6
envelopes.signOff: to be continued, Episode 7 is coming

paragraphs.text: hiii
And finally, as I was walking toward the bus, we had our last conversation and I gave her a hug. How can I even explain that hug to someone? Then I boarded the train and headed back home.

During the journey, she told me, “You have to send me snaps and talk to me every day,” or whenever I found the time. I agreed to her request. After reaching home, I started teasing her and making fun; I really love teasing her and having a laugh together.

While I was on the train, I’d create situations just to make her shout at me or “gussa dilana,” like telling her I was doing stunts on the moving train and things like that. Around that time, I gave her my father’s number. She used to say, “If you do anything stupid, I will message your father.” We were spending such a good time together.

Then, I received my internship letter—the confirmation that I would be coming at the end of August. Before that, it was only a 50-50 chance, but now it was 100 percent certain. We talked whenever we got the chance. She used to send me snaps of her doing something crazy every day, and she expected me to send snaps back. But I wasn’t sending them because I just don’t like sending snaps.

When she’d tell me about things we should do when I returned, I used to say, “Don’t expect anything from me, I always end up hurting people.” Many times we had this kind of conversation, and she would say, “You are very selfish and you only think about yourself.” I would just give those kinds of answers, saying I’d only think about my personal interests and what I like.

Somehow, we kept the chat going. When she used to send “I love you” messages, I would divert the topic to something else or just say “love you” back. She’d send messages in the morning with hearts, and I’d just send a normal message in return. She used to scold me during the day, and I would correct it later; that’s how we spent our time with small fights and beautiful conversations.

We were actually loving the long distance and used to call every day. Soon, I shifted to Indore for my internship, which started on 25 May 2026. Then, my days became blocked because phones weren't allowed, so I wasn't able to talk to her from morning until 6 in the evening.

I didn't get the chance to talk then, but in the evening, she’d somehow find a way to reach me. We had our conversations then. I didn't tell her exactly when I was coming back because I wanted to give her a surprise. My first day there was a trap, and it got as worse as it could be.

But in the evening, I’d come home and talk to her, and all the stress would just be relieved. I had a friend who came for the internship with me as well, but I’d usually leave her and just get home as soon as possible so I could talk to my girl.
During that time, the person I was staying with said, “I am going on leave; when I come back, you should search for a new home or hostel.” I said okay. He was going for two weeks. I was supposed to come back on 7 June 2026, but then he extended it to 11 June.
During this, my internship workload was also increasing. Whenever I got the chance, I talked to her, and it was the best part of my whole day. Even then, she used to send those beautiful messages like “I love you” or kissing emojis, and I was still replying in a not-so-good manner. Then the third week of the internship arrived.

Then the third week of the internship arrived.
Before that, I met a friend from my school days. He asked me, “Do you have a girlfriend or are you single?” I told him, “Yes, I have a girlfriend,” and shared the complete story. Then he asked how I proposed to her. I showed him the lines I used to express my love. He told me his own story, and after that day, I realized something: I wasn't doing it right.
I have to express my love; I have to tell her and propose properly because she is loving me so much. I started thinking I would tell her everything, but then the same confusion started to happen.
During that time, the work pressure began to increase, and I wasn’t able to think about anything other than work. I was feeling numb because of the pressure. I was new to the job, and yet I was still loved by her in the same way she always had. And then the day happened and……….

envelopes.title: Envelope 7
envelopes.signOff: to be continued, Episode 8 is coming

paragraphs.text: hiii

And then this was the third week of my internship. I had received my project, and every evening my guide would inquire about my progress and what I had accomplished throughout the day. I would explain my work to him, but he wasn’t satisfied and started shouting at me right in the middle of the hall. He told me to leave, so I headed home.
At the same time, I was out searching for a hostel, and my parents were also pressuring me about things. I had a long list of work to finish that day. As soon as I got home and started looking for hostels, she messaged me. I just said okay, and when she asked if I was busy, I kept giving those same kinds of short answers, like “okay, okay,” to every message.

Then, when I asked her to call or when she tried to call, my father would call me to ask about things, and everything got messed up again. That day, when everything finally ended—after I reached home, went for dinner, and came back—she called me. We started talking, and she asked me, “What do you think about me? Do you like me or not?” She asked if I was serious about her or if I just liked talking to her. And then, that day, everything became a mess.
The next day, it was the same thing again, but I was a bit more free and doing some work at home. She asked me the same questions, and my mind was completely blank; I wasn’t thinking. Then she said, “I think we should just stay as friends,” and my mind stopped working. I told her, “We are friends now, what else is there to become?” Her face fell, and all her expectations and everything seemed lost.

In the evening, I called her again to try and resolve the problem, but I still couldn't express myself at all. I was trying to explain my plans, but that only made things worse. She said, “Then I’ll just have to listen to whatever my mummy says,” and I replied that if nothing happens in the future, then that’s how it will be in the end. During the call, I was telling her that I only ever get unconditional love from my boys and my team, which only made her feel more down.
Then she started to cry, and I told her not to cry for me or anyone else. This is how everything got messed up; as I was talking to her about us, the same thing happened—I wasn’t doing well, and with all that pressure, I started to ruin everything. Finally, she said, “Let’s just be friends,” or asked if I didn’t want to talk, and I said, “As you want.” Whenever she asked something, I used to say, “Do whatever feels good to you.” Then she said, “Let’s be friends,” and I said yes to her without any questions . and at that time my bain was not even workin  .And then, the hardest time of my internship began.

envelopes.title: Envelope 8
envelopes.signOff: to be continued, Episode 9 is coming

paragraphs.text: hiiii

During that time, the weight of the pressure started to get to me, and I became so tensed that I didn’t know how to handle anything. When she’d call and we’d talk, I’d find myself completely out of context, unable to remember what we were even discussing. Because of this behavior, she started getting angry with me.
My days would begin with the internship—having those rough, "dirty" days—and when I’d come home to talk, she’d started losing interest and wasn’t talking the way she used to. I made an effort, a "koshish," to bring her back to how she was, but I could feel the gap growing between us. It felt like she was just being a friend; I’d lost that emotional touch with her, and by the time I realized it, she was already far away.

When I tried to make her happy again, I gave her stupid reasons, acting like everyone else was doing the same to me. I started doing foolish things, even a bit of blackmailing in the process, and "manane ki koshish mein aur kharab karta chala gaya."
Then the first half of my internship ended, and I was free—no pressure, no workload, everything was over. I started to realize what had happened to me in that one month. The first thing I did was reply to all those messages and her questions. I told her, "I really like you and love being with you, but I’m clear about the future. I love spending time with you." But by then, it was all too late.

I spent a week at home, but as soon as I returned, it all started again—getting numb and depressed, losing the taste for everything. One day, while talking to my father, he asked, "Awaaz dheere aa rahi hai, kya hua?" I said, "Kuch nahi."
Then he began telling me things a working person should know: "Your work stays at the office. If you take it home, it will destroy you. In the process of trying to make someone happy, you’ll lose your own smile." He told me to be happy and do what I like—what makes me happy—and if I couldn’t do it, then just leave it.
He gave me motivation, saying, "Aage kya hoga use apne ko kya, aaj khush reh na. Aaj hi tu aise rahega, itna pressure lega toh kuch nahi hoga, just you will destroy you." After that, I realized that my biggest happiness, my biggest support, my "burry," my sweetheart—I had just hurt her very much. Since that day, I haven’t done a single day’s work at home, and I’ve learned these lessons

Actions are better than words; words can be fake, but actions aren’t. This was one of my biggest myths. Words are very necessary. She was just asking for a snap or an “I love you” message, and I failed to give her that. By saying “I love you,” it really reveals the affection and expression of love. You’ll also feel the love and be happy that you’re with someone who makes you happy.
Girls are emotional: yes, they are. They are the spice of your life and give it taste. I had that taste, but I made her feel bad and not worth it. I didn’t give her the credit she deserved.
Expressing love: yes, she is yours, so let her know you love her every time. Say “I love you” back; it helps make her happy. Your actions, like giving surprises, are the way to show it. Please express your love to her—that makes her happy, and if she’s happy, she’ll make you happy too. I tossed her roses in the wrong direction; I always should have given her the same love she gave me, but I failed to do so.
Your work gives you money, not happiness; only relationships give you that. The only people in my life who have given me true happiness are my mummy, papa, and Nainsi. The unconditional love she gave me, no one else can provide. I always want to be with her; I can’t imagine myself with anyone else. And leave the the wok it your work place only .

Expressing love properly: I learned this the hard way, so please, tell your special someone that you love them.


	I just couldn't bring myself to say “I love you” to Nainsi, and I even failed at sending her a simple snap. All she wanted were those little things—a sweet voice note or just a message—but I couldn't even do that. She is honestly one of the most incredible people I've ever known. I really think I should have stayed for those four extra days; I truly regret leaving her early.

hiii,

I am really sorry, yaar, seriously sorry for every single thing. I just wanted to share this with you from my heart there isn’t even a song in this yet, but please just play it and I really, really love you.
	Please just give me a chance; I love you even more than all you have done for me. I want to give you all that love back—the same love you have given me—but with even more love.
